mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses equity prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and acceleration in consumer price inflation compresses real household purchasing power, forcing equity deleveraging and depressing forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns more than 55% of the time when both proxies move in the specified direction, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, accelerating personal savings withdrawals indicate consumption exhaustion, which precedes earnings downgrades and generates negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 55% of observations when RSAFS declines, or the rolling 3-year correlation between RSAFS changes and SPX returns exceeds 0.10 in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.50 percent, the market prices in an imminent policy pivot toward tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative short-term forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces a positive hit rate below 50% for negative returns over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the average 5-day forward return after crossing the threshold is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong nonfarm payroll growth and rising housing starts reflect robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of co-occurring instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy supply chains, which supports corporate margins and generates positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year information coefficient drops below 0.02, or forward 20-day returns are not significantly higher during periods of positive INDPRO growth compared to contraction periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rapid drawsdowns in real savings act as a leading indicator of consumption stress, causing immediate equity risk repricing and negative short-term forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite falling savings, or the signal's predictive power disappears when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized.
